hawkish_phrases: the risks to the inflation outlook were tilted to the upside, especially in the near term
markets were pricing in rate hikes of about 40 basis points by the end of 2026
A prolonged war could lead to a larger and longer-lasting upward shift in energy prices than expected
medium-term inflation expectations might be more fragile now and could increase more quickly than in the past
would not hesitate to act if there were a threat to its price stability objective
dovish_phrases: the economy was now expected to expand at a slower pace in the short term than previously envisaged
growth had been revised down by 0.3 percentage points for 2026
euro area two-year real rates had declined, reflecting a downward revision to economic growth
inflation could turn out to be lower if the economic repercussions of the war in the Middle East proved to be more short-lived
summary: The Governing Council unanimously held all three key ECB interest rates unchanged amid war-induced uncertainty creating upside inflation risks and downside growth risks, with communication emphasizing readiness to act if medium-term price stability is threatened while maintaining optionality through a meeting-by-meeting approach.
